items.headline: Los Gatos to hold municipal election November 3, 2026
items.summary: City Council adopted a resolution calling a general municipal election for November 3, 2026, for three seats.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard development
items.summary: City Council approved the final subdivision map for Tract 10675 Boulevard, moving the project toward development.

items.headline: Transit-oriented housing development rules adopted for Milpitas
items.summary: The City Council approved new zoning allowing housing development as a permitted use at transit-oriented stops, with Tier 1 and Tier 2 stops identified within Milpitas. This streamlines approval for residential projects near transit access without case-by-case discretionary review.

items.headline: 68-unit apartment complex permitted on East St John Street
items.summary: A $10.1 million apartment building with parking garage received a permit at 700 E St John St. This is the only multifamily housing project in the batch and adds the most units of any single development.

items.headline: Homeowner seeks variance to reduce rear yard setback for addition
items.summary: A homeowner at an unspecified location is requesting approval to build a 730-square-foot first-story addition to a single-family home that would require reducing the rear yard setback from the required 10 feet to 7 feet 4 inches, and increasing rear yard encroachment to 34 percent where 25 percent is the maximum allowed. The Planning Commission will review the design and variance request.

items.headline: City Council to adopt Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan
items.summary: The council will vote on a new strategic plan for Mountain View's parks and recreation services. The plan details priorities and direction for the city's park system and recreational programs.

items.headline: Council to discuss City Decarbonization Goal and Five-Year Plan
items.summary: A study session scheduled for 5:00 PM will review Mountain View's decarbonization goal and a five-year action plan to reduce emissions across city operations and community activities.

items.headline: City approves $1.6M California Highway Patrol agreement for FIFA World Cup traffic
items.summary: Santa Clara City Council and Stadium Authority Board approved a standard agreement with the California Highway Patrol for traffic management services during the FIFA World Cup, with a projected cost of $1.6 million and related budget amendment.
